hero.quote: By the end of the evening, we hope you'll feel the taste of our traditions.
hero.headline: From Karakol with Love

about.subtitle: Our Philosophy
about.title: The Spirit of Nomadic Hospitality
about.description: Dastarkhan is not just a table with food. It is a centuries-old tradition of respect, the center of the home, and a place where the history of the great nomads of Central Asia unfolds through an open heart and authentic flavors. Every dish at Dastorkon is prepared according to carefully preserved recipes, conveying the true mosaic of our ancestors' flavors.
about.cardsTitle: PERUSE MENUS, BOOK TABLES, EXPLORE GIFTS AND SUNDRIES

menuPreview.subtitle: Cuisine
menuPreview.title: Nomadic Taste Traditions
menuPreview.description: Every dish at Dastorkon is a journey through the centuries. We prepare plov, manti, and beshbarmak using authentic recipes, local farm products, and traditional spices, carefully preserving the gastronomic heritage of our ancestors.

interior.subtitle: Atmosphere
interior.title: Restaurant Interior
interior.description: The interior of Dastorkon embodies the patterns of history, daily life, and traditions of nomadic peoples. Every detail — from the carved tyundyuk on the ceiling to the authentic antler lamps — showcases tribal kinship, nomadic hospitality, and the great spirit of freedom.

contacts.subtitle: Our doors are open
contacts.title: We look forward to welcoming you
contacts.description: Dastorkon is the warmth of a mountain home, tradition, and love for our guests. Come and share a piece of Karakol's soul with us, feeling the comfort where every guest is the closest one.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.intro: Dastorkon Etno-Cafe ("we", "our", or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your personal information when you visit our website or use our services.
privacyPolicy.whatWeCollectText: We may collect the following types of information: name, phone number, email address, reservation details (date, time, number of guests), and any messages or special requests you provide through our booking form.
privacyPolicy.howWeUseText: We use your information to process and confirm reservations, communicate with you about your bookings, respond to your inquiries, improve our services, and send you relevant updates if you have opted in.
privacyPolicy.sharingText: We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties. We may share your data with trusted service providers who assist us in operating our website or business, provided they agree to keep your information confidential.
privacyPolicy.cookiesText: Our website uses cookies to enhance your browsing experience. You can control cookie settings through your browser preferences. For more details, please see our Cookie Policy.
privacyPolicy.yourRightsText: You have the right to access, update, or delete your personal information at any time. To exercise these rights, please contact us using the details provided in the Contacts section of our website.
privacyPolicy.contactText: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us by phone or through our website.

cookiePolicy.title: Cookie Policy
cookiePolicy.intro: This Cookie Policy explains how Dastorkon Etno-Cafe uses cookies and similar technologies on our website.
cookiePolicy.whatAreCookiesText: Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites work more efficiently, as well as to provide information to the site owners.
cookiePolicy.howWeUseCookiesText: We use essential cookies to ensure the proper functioning of our website, including session management, language preferences, and reservation form functionality. We also use analytics cookies to understand how visitors interact with our site, which helps us improve your experience.
cookiePolicy.essentialText: These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions you make such as setting your language preferences or filling in forms.
cookiePolicy.analyticsText: These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and anonymous.
cookiePolicy.managingText: Most web browsers allow you to control cookies through their settings preferences. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site may not work as a result. For more information, visit your browser's help pages.
cookiePolicy.contactText: If you have any questions about our use of cookies, please contact us through our website.

menuPage.category_desc: Immerse yourself in the atmosphere of nomadic aesthetics with our signature dishes, prepared according to ancient recipes using the freshest local ingredients.

items.title: Samarkand Plov
items.description: Traditional Uzbek plov made with devzira rice, tender lamb, yellow carrots, and aromatic spices.

items.title: Mariestan Dip Set
items.description: An assortment of Central Asian dips served with crispy tandoor flatbreads.

items.title: Special Sauces
items.description: Tangy tomato and garlic sauces to complement meat dishes.

items.title: Dinper Sauce
items.description: A secret recipe from our chef made with aromatic herbs.

bookPage.subtitle: Reservation
bookPage.title: Book a Table
bookPage.description: Please fill out the form below, and we will contact you to confirm your reservation.
bookPage.success_desc: Your request has been successfully sent. We will contact you shortly.
bookPage.error_msg: An error occurred. Please try again later.

cuisine.subtitle: Cuisine
cuisine.title: A Taste of the Great Steppe
cuisine.description: At Dastorkon, every dish is a story told through fire, spice, and centuries-old traditions. Our chefs honor the nomadic heritage with slow-cooked meats, hand-rolled noodles, and fragrant pilafs — each plate a journey across the Kyrgyz mountains and Central Asian plains.

menu.title: Our Menu
menu.description: From fragrant plov to delicate manti — authentic tastes of Central Asia.

book.title: Reserve a Table
book.description: Reserve your table for an intimate dinner or a grand family celebration.

events.title: Events & Banquets
events.description: Catering and banquets — off-site or in our authentic ethno-interiors.

cart.fillRequired: Please fill in all required fields
cart.orderError: An error occurred. Please try again later.
cart.successDesc: We have received your order and will contact you shortly for confirmation.

eventsHub.banquetsDesc: Host an unforgettable celebration in our luxurious halls. The perfect atmosphere, authentic ethno‑interior, and first‑class service for weddings, anniversaries, and family gatherings.
eventsHub.cateringDesc: High-quality national cuisine wherever you wish. Professional off-site catering for events of any scale, preserving premium presentation and service.

banquets.title: Banquets
banquets.desc: Grand feasts in ethno-style. We set a dastarkhan for any number of guests. Traditional dishes, live music, national flavor.

sherine.title: Sherine
sherine.desc: Intimate gatherings with tasting of oriental sweets. Perfect for getting to know the culture through taste.

birthdays.title: Birthdays
birthdays.desc: Give yourself and your guests a celebration in a unique atmosphere. Complimentary dessert from the house for the birthday person.

matchmaking.title: Matchmaking — Sөykө saluu
matchmaking.desc: Traditional ceremony of family introduction. We create an authentic setting for an important chapter of your story.

parentMeeting.title: Parent Meeting
parentMeeting.desc: A cozy dinner for two families. A neutral, warm atmosphere so everyone feels at home.

kidsParty.title: Children's Parties — Tushoo toy
kidsParty.desc: A baby's first step is a big celebration. Bright emotions, national traditions, and treats for children and adults.

anyEvent.title: Any Event
anyEvent.desc: Corporate events, anniversaries, creative evenings. We'll discuss the format and create a menu for your request.

cookieConsent.title: We use cookies
cookieConsent.description: Dastorkon uses essential cookies for the proper functioning of the website, saving language preferences, and ensuring reservation functionality. We do not store clients' personal data and do not share it with third parties.

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.description: Looks like you've wandered into the nomad steppe. This page doesn't exist.

storyPage.heroTitle: Where the mountain meets the table
storyPage.heroLead: Dastorkon was born in Karakol — a town at the foot of the Tien Shan, where hospitality is not ceremony, but the air people breathe.
storyPage.inlineAfter: for everyone who arrives with an open heart.
storyPage.bentoLead: Not decor, not a legend on the wall — the living rhythm of a house that calls its own.

dastarkhan.title: Dastarkhan
dastarkhan.text: The table as a ritual of respect. A seat at the cloth means you are a welcome guest, not a passerby.

fire.title: Fire and patience
fire.text: Plov, slow meat, broth — dishes that refuse haste. We cook as the mountain taught: slowly, honestly.

karakol.title: Karakol
karakol.text: A town of travelers, fishermen, and family feasts. Our home is at 107 Przhevalsky, in the heart of that story.

craft.title: Hands of makers
craft.text: From the tyundyuk above to the pattern on the plate — every detail recalls: nomadic beauty was never accidental.

table.title: A shared table
table.text: Family, friends, a banquet, or a quiet dinner for two — one rule: the table is never empty for the soul.

roots.title: Roots
roots.text: Dastorkon is a word that holds a culture: a laid table, generosity, respect for the guest. We took the name not as branding, but as a promise.

kitchen.title: Kitchen
kitchen.text: Recipes travel from memory, not trends. Beshbarmak, manti, plov — flavors that remember the steppe, the fire, and the long road home.

house.title: House
house.text: The interior is not an ethno theme. It is warm light, carved ornament, mountain quiet beyond the window, and voices that return again and again.

guest.title: Guest
guest.text: In tradition, the guest is sacred. We do not merely serve — we welcome. By evening's end, we want you to carry a taste, not just a bill.

storyPage.pinLead: Four pillars Dastorkon stands on — without them it would be just another restaurant.

recipe.title: Recipe as inheritance
recipe.text: We do not simplify dishes for fashion. If tradition needs time — we give it time.

local.title: Local produce
local.text: Mountains, pastures, season — the plate should taste of the Issyk-Kul region, not a warehouse of shortcuts.

ritual.title: The ritual of welcome
ritual.text: From the first greeting to the last pour of tea — the evening builds like a conversation, not a ticket queue.

feast.title: Celebration without falsehood
feast.text: Banquet, catering, family table — different scale, one essence: a true dastorkon.

arrive.title: You enter
arrive.text: The scent of plov, soft light, ornament on the walls. The town stays outside — another rhythm begins.

share.title: You share the table
share.text: Dishes arrive to be shared. Conversation lengthens. Time stops being a schedule.

taste.title: You taste a story
taste.text: Each flavor is a road: fire, spice, hands that remember how to feed their own.

leave.title: You leave full
leave.text: Not only in body. A good dastarkhan leaves warmth — and the wish to return.

q1.text: It felt less like a restaurant and more like a house that had been waiting for us. Plov like grandmother's — only more beautiful.
q1.author: Aigul, Bishkek

q2.text: We came after the trek to Ala-Kul. Dinner at Dastorkon was the perfect end of the route — warm, honest, mountain-true.
q2.author: Marco, Berlin

q3.text: We celebrated a family anniversary. Hall, service, kitchen — everything held the feast without empty show.
q3.author: The Zhumabekov family, Karakol

storyPage.ctaTitle: Your place at the dastorkon is waiting
storyPage.ctaText: Come share the table — on a weekday, a celebration, after a mountain day. Karakol tastes better when you sit with us.
